### Post by That_Geek on 2006-12-12
Ok, big problems here, whenever i install any drivers for my nvidia fx 5200 ultra, it doesnt work, well, the drivers install alright, and then i go into my xorg.conf 

here it is (with the nvidia drivers on it) 

```
 # /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.

# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

	Mode	0666

```

and when i am in there, i put "nvidia" into the driver section, and then i restart X

when it boots up, instead of getting login screen like normal, it just stays on this weird black/blank screen that never goes away

i have tried both the nvidia-glx and the nvidia-glx-legacy

im outta options! 

i dont know what i am doing wrong!

can anyone help?

Thanks in advance

---

### Post by tajreed on 2006-12-12
Start over and put in the NV driver. Run sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg and select the nv driver. Leave everything else at default.

---

### Post by That_Geek on 2006-12-12
ok, did that, now what?

(just so we are clear, using X is not a prob, normally i just use the vesa drivers, but i want to play some ET, so i need 3d rendering)

---

### Post by tajreed on 2006-12-13
Next thing I would do would be to make sure your Nvidia card is supported by the new drivers. This can be done at the Nvidia site. Then I used the Automatix script to install the latest prop. driver.

---
